core_mechanism: A parameter-dependent quotient construction: forming the quotient of a linear space by a (nonabelian) group action with a tunable stability/moment-map parameter, whose distinct chambers ('phases') are topologically different quotient spaces separated by walls, so that varying the parameter drives transitions between geometries that need not be related by an invertible (birational) map but by a deeper equivalence.
brief_reason: The abstract builds target spaces (flag manifolds, Calabi-Yau intersections) as gauged linear sigma models whose Kahler 'phases' change with a parameter and are 'not birational' but instead related by Kuznetsov's homological projective duality, i.e. a stability-parameter-driven group-quotient (GIT/symplectic-reduction) construction with wall-separated phases.
